skill.name: azure-reliability
skill.description: Assess and improve the reliability posture of PaaS Applications (Azure Functions and Azure App Service). Scans deployed resources for zone redundancy, ZRS storage, health probes, and multi-region failover. Presents a feature-pivoted checklist, then drives staged remediation (CLI or IaC patches) end-to-end with user confirmation. WHEN: "assess reliability", "check reliability", "zone redundant", "multi-region failover", "high availability", "disaster recovery", "single points of failure", "reliability posture", "resiliency".
skill.summary: Assesses and improves Azure PaaS reliability through zone redundancy, storage replication, health probes, and multi-region failover checks.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 509 potential issues across 13 markdown documentation files. After evaluation, all findings are false positives: the 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns are markdown formatting backticks in Azure CLI examples, 'weak cryptographic' references are documentation text about encryption standards, and 'hardcoded URL' entries are Microsoft Learn documentation links. No executable code is present in this skill. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: External command patterns are documentation examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis detected 436 'external_commands' patterns. All instances are markdown backtick code formatting containing Azure CLI commands (az, azd) shown as documentation examples. No actual code execution occurs.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs are Microsoft documentation references
low_findings.description: Static analysis detected 10 'hardcoded URL' patterns. All are legitimate links to Microsoft Learn documentation (learn.microsoft.com) used as reference material in the skill's guidance.

low_findings.title: Filesystem path references are documentation examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis detected 3 filesystem path patterns. These are example paths in documentation showing Bicep/Terraform file structures, not actual filesystem access.

low_findings.title: Dynamic function constructor in documentation
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a 'Dynamic function constructor' pattern. This is a reference to Azure Functions programming model documentation, not actual code execution.

content.value_statement: Azure PaaS applications often lack zone redundancy and multi-region failover configurations, creating single points of failure. This skill systematically scans Azure Functions and App Service deployments, identifies reliability gaps, and guides remediation through CLI commands or IaC patches.
actual_capabilities: Scans Azure App Service and Functions deployments for zone redundancy configuration
Checks storage accounts for zone-redundant storage (ZRS) replication settings
Verifies health probe configurations on App Service applications
Assesses multi-region failover readiness and traffic manager setup
Generates feature-pivoted reliability checklists with pass/fail status
Provides staged remediation via Azure CLI commands or Bicep/Terraform IaC patches with user confirmation
limitations: Focused on Azure App Service and Azure Functions only (not VMs, AKS, or other compute)
Does not perform automated remediation without explicit user confirmation at each step
Requires Azure CLI authentication and appropriate subscription access permissions
IaC patching supports Bicep and Terraform but may not handle complex custom module structures

use_cases.title: Pre-deployment reliability audit
use_cases.description: DevOps engineers run a reliability check on a new App Service deployment to ensure zone redundancy and proper health probes are configured before going live.

use_cases.title: Disaster recovery assessment
use_cases.description: Cloud architects evaluate an existing production application's disaster recovery posture, identifying single points of failure and multi-region gaps.

use_cases.title: Compliance and best practices review
use_cases.description: SRE teams systematically review all PaaS applications against Azure Well-Architected Framework reliability pillar recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic reliability check
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess the reliability posture of my Azure App Service application named 'my-webapp' in the 'production-rg' resource group.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick health check for a single application

prompt_templates.title: Multi-region failover review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the multi-region failover configuration for all Azure Functions in subscription 'PROD-SUB'. Check Traffic Manager, paired region deployments, and storage replication.

prompt_templates.title: Storage redundancy audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit all storage accounts in resource group 'data-rg' for zone-redundant storage (ZRS) configuration. Flag any accounts using LRS that contain critical data.

prompt_templates.title: Full remediation workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a complete reliability assessment on my PaaS applications, then generate Bicep patches to remediate all findings. Show me the changes before applying them.
prompt_templates.scenario: End-to-end assessment and remediation with IaC

output_examples.input: Check reliability of App Service 'api-gateway' in resource group 'prod-rg'
output: Zone Redundancy: FAIL - App Service plan is configured for 1 instance (no zone redundancy)
Health Probes: PASS - Health check endpoint configured at /health
Storage: PASS - Dependent storage account uses GZRS
Multi-Region: WARNING - No secondary region deployment detected
Recommendation: Scale to 3+ instances across availability zones, consider deploying to paired region 'westus2'

output_examples.input: Assess storage accounts in 'data-rg' for ZRS configuration
output: Non-Compliant: 3 accounts (media, backups, archive) - using LRS
Risk: Data loss possible if single datacenter fails
Suggested Action: Migrate to ZRS using 'az storage account update --sku Standard_ZRS'

best_practices: Always run a full assessment before applying any remediation patches to understand the complete reliability posture
Review each suggested change carefully and apply remediations in staging environments before production
Use IaC patches (Bicep/Terraform) for remediation to maintain infrastructure-as-code consistency and version control
anti_patterns: Do not apply remediation commands without user confirmation at each step
Avoid skipping the assessment phase and jumping directly to remediation commands
Do not use this skill on production environments without first testing in a development or staging subscription

faq.question: What Azure services does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on Azure App Service and Azure Functions. It also checks dependent resources like storage accounts and traffic managers.

faq.question: Does the skill automatically fix reliability issues?
faq.answer: No. The skill presents findings and proposed remediations, then waits for user confirmation before applying any changes.

faq.question: What remediation methods are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports Azure CLI commands and Infrastructure-as-Code patches in both Bicep and Terraform formats.

faq.question: What permissions are required to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need read access to the target resource groups and write access if you want to apply remediations. The skill uses Azure CLI authentication.

faq.question: Can the skill handle complex multi-subscription deployments?
faq.answer: The skill works within a single subscription context. For multi-subscription assessments, run separate checks for each subscription.

faq.question: How does the skill handle resources it cannot assess?
faq.answer: Unsupported resource types are skipped with a note in the assessment report. The skill focuses on App Service, Functions, and their direct dependencies.
